name: Command A
archDisplay: Dense transformer (111B) with hybrid sliding-window/full attention (3:1 ratio) and GQA
note: 111B enterprise flagship, 256K context, runs on just two GPUs (A100/H100); rivals GPT-4o and DeepSeek-V3 on agentic enterprise tasks.
tpsNote: Requires ~2x A100/H100 GPUs for practical serving; does not fit on a single RTX 4090 even quantized

results.notes: Command A technical report (arXiv 2504.00698), Table 1, page 2, 'Code' capability row, Bird-SQL column. Command A scores 59.5 vs. DeepSeek-V3 53.1, GPT-4o 50.5, Llama 3.3 70B 58.0. Directly re-read the table image from the downloaded PDF (page 2) to confirm this number, which is not currently in the corpus's results array.

verificationNotes: Fully confirmed: release date March 13, 2025 verified via HPCwire and Cohere's own blog (cohere.com/blog/command-a); HF model card + arXiv paper confirm 111B params, 256K context, CC-BY-NC + AUP license, deployable on 2x A100/H100. Downloaded and read the actual PDF of arXiv 2504.00698 Table 1 directly — all seven Command A benchmark numbers (MMLU 85.5, MATH 80.0, IFEval 90.9, GPQA 50.8, Taubench 51.7, MBPP+ 86.2, BFCL 63.8) appear verbatim. Pricing ($2.50/$10 per 1M) confirmed via cohere.com/pricing-derived aggregators. No changes made.
